name: Covão dos Conchos Trail
name_original: Trilho do Covão dos Conchos — Serra da Estrela
description: Hike the moderate 5.5-mile Covão dos Conchos Trail in Serra da Estrela to see the unique bell-mouth spillway and stunning mountain views.
region_intro: Hiking around Albufeira de Lakea Comprida offers trails through a landscape shaped by glacial erosion, featuring a large high-altitude reservoir and unique geological formations. The region is characterized by its wide depression of glacial origin, polished rock surfaces, and fields of erratic boulders. Located at approximately 1,600 meters, the area provides access to the largest water reservoir in the Serra da Estrela. This environment presents a variety of hiking opportunities across different terrains. Best hiking trails around Albufeira de Lakea Comprida The most popular hiking route is Covão dos Conchos Trail, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the unique Covão dos Conchos spillway. Another top favourite among local hikers is Covão dos Conchos and Lagoa Comprida Loop, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This trail features five lagoons and the Covão dos Conchos spillway. Local hikers also love the Long Lake – Covão dos Conchos Trail loop from Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail leading through glacial landscapes and offering significant elevation gain, often completed in about 6 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Albufeira de Lakea Comprida is defined by glacial valleys, high-altitude reservoirs, and unique rock formations. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Albufeira de Lakea Comprida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Albufeira de Lakea Comprida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents through glacial landscapes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the hiking trails in this region?
A: The trails around Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida cater to various abilities. You can find approximately 18 easy routes, 28 moderate trails, and 11 more difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable hike for almost everyone.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails near Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida?
A: Yes, there are several family-friendly options. For an easier walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake loop from Albufeira do Covão do Forno&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.6 km long and takes around an hour. It's a great way to experience the high-altitude environment without a strenuous climb.

Q: What unique natural features can I expect to see while hiking here?
A: The region is renowned for its unique glacial landscapes. You'll encounter polished rock surfaces, fields of erratic boulders transported by ancient glaciers, and the vast Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida itself, which is the largest water reservoir in the Serra da Estrela. A must-see is the intriguing Covão dos Conchos spillway, a bell-mouth shaped drainage structure.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida?
A: The best time for hiking is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October) when the weather is milder and trails are clear. During winter, the area can experience snow and ice, making some routes more challenging and requiring alpine experience and proper gear.

Q: What should I wear or bring when hiking in this high-altitude area?
A: Given the altitude of approximately 1,600 meters, weather conditions can change rapidly. It's advisable to bring layers, including a windbreaker, even on sunny days. Always carry sufficient water, as water sources can be scarce on higher sections. Good hiking boots are essential, and for some trails, especially beyond Covão dos Conchos, good orientation skills or a GPS device are recommended due to stone markers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida?
A: Yes, many trails are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2175482092/covao-dos-conchos-and-lagoa-comprida-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos and Lagoa Comprida Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 11.1 km route featuring five lagoons and the famous spillway. For a longer adventure, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/504535" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake – Covão dos Conchos Trail loop from Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park when visiting Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida for a hike?
A: Parking is generally available near the Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida dam, which serves as a common starting point for many trails in the area. Specific parking instructions or designated areas might be signposted locally.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Albufeira de Lagoa Comprida?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain views, the unique glacial landscapes, and the fascinating Covão dos Conchos spillway. The variety of trails also allows for different experiences, from serene lakeside walks to more challenging mountain treks.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on hiking trails in the Serra da Estrela region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, as natural water sources can be limited, particularly in drier months.

Q: Can I visit Covão dos Conchos as part of a hike?
A: Absolutely! The Covão dos Conchos spillway is a major highlight and accessible via several trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2147148935/covao-dos-conchos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9 km route specifically leading to this unique engineering marvel, taking about 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any easy routes for beginners or those looking for a short walk?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32162441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Quelhas Lagoon loop from Nascente Rio Zêzere&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.3 km trail with moderate elevation gain, offering a pleasant walk through the landscape.
tips:
Located at an altitude of approximately 1,600 meters, Lagoa Comprida is the largest water reservoir in the Serra da Estrela, intended for hydroelectric generation.

This natural lake lies in a wide depression of glacial origin, approximately one kilometer long, which fed the Covão Grande glacier. Numerous glacial scars are visible along its banks, making this lake one of the most interesting geomorphological sites in the Serra da Estrela.

Construction on this lake began in 1911, and by 1914, it was already six meters high. Later, in 1965, it underwent heightening work that increased its height to 28 meters, currently boasting a storage capacity of approximately 14 cubic hectometers, flooding an area of approximately 80 hectares.

Small streams of water flow into this lagoon, as well as water retained in the Covões dos Conchos and Covões do Meio and carried to this lagoon through underground tunnels.

We meet a lot of people on the route, especially on August 16, the trail is easy but many areas of scree require good shoes and flexible ankles. The landscapes are magnificent with the blue sky and the heat is tempered by the altitude. The short swim in the water reservoir of the dam allows you to relax after the walk.

Covão dos Conchos is one of those places that seems to have come from a parallel universe. At first glance, it is a mysterious hole in the middle of the lake, a funnel that seems to swallow the waters in mesmerizing silence. But this phenomenon is not the work of nature, but rather of human engineering: it is a tunnel built in the 1950s to divert the waters of Ribeira das Naves to Lagoa Comprida, the largest reservoir in Serra da Estrela, where it supplies hydroelectric power.

Interestingly, in the driest summers, the water level of the lake can drop so much that the tunnel becomes visible, as if patiently awaiting the next flood.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Covão dos Conchos Trail?
faqs.answer: The round trip typically takes about 2 to 3 hours to complete at a steady pace. The route is approximately 8.86 kilometers (about 5.5 miles) long.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is situated at around 1500 meters above sea level and features a dirt road with sections of loose stones and rocky patches. While largely flat, there's a gentle ascent to the highest point before descending to Covão dos Conchos. Good, sturdy shoes are highly recommended due to the uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Covão dos Conchos Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's generally considered an easy to moderate hike, suitable for most fitness levels, including families with children. However, some sections with loose stones require sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is the unique bell-shaped spillway of Covão dos Conchos. You'll also pass by Long Lake (Lagoa Comprida), where the trail often starts, and enjoy impressive mountain landscapes with grey granite boulders and glacial lakes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Lagoa Comprida. There is parking available at Lagoa Comprida, but it can fill up quickly, especially during peak season. Arriving early is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit Covão dos Conchos?
faqs.answer: The trail offers breathtaking landscapes year-round. During warmer months, you'll experience vast open skies and diverse vegetation. In winter, the area can be covered in snow, offering a different, striking experience, but accessibility might be limited due to weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Covão dos Conchos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Serra da Estrela Natural Park, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and other hikers. Ensure your dog is comfortable with rocky terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail or Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Covão dos Conchos Trail or to access Serra da Estrela Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and guidelines for protected areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA PR07] Torre * San Romao and the [CIMA PR07] Torre * Seia trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for the hike?
faqs.answer: Given the exposed nature of the trail with very little shade, it's crucial to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and sturdy hiking shoes. Layers of clothing are also recommended as mountain weather can change quickly.

faqs.question: Is the trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Covão dos Conchos Trail is typically an out-and-back route, starting and ending at Lagoa Comprida. You hike to the Covão dos Conchos spillway and then return the same way.
